Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search Airtable records using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Assigned Coordinator", value: "Javier Morales") to find all relocations assigned to Javier. The results include: Kevin Tran / Vectral Systems (Portland to Denver, Completed), Danielle Osei / Vectral Systems (Phoenix to Denver, one record shows Completed and another shows Cancelled), Rachel Dominguez / Canopy Health (Los Angeles to Portland, In Progress), Sasha Greenwald / PivotPoint Labs (New York to Nashville, In Progress), Jordan Whitfield / BrightLoop Analytics (Minneapolis to Denver, In Progress), Marcus Rivera / Sunbelt Venture Partners (Miami to Denver, Completed), Lisa Petrosky / NorthWind Technologies (Indianapolis to Chicago, Completed), Sasha Petrov / StormCloud Analytics (Denver to Austin, Completed), Ben Schaefer / NorthWind Technologies (Milwaukee to Chicago, Completed), Ravi Sundaram / Tideway Hospitality Tech (Tampa to Denver, Completed), Tyler Okonkwo / Canopy Health (Philadelphia to Denver, In Progress), Priya Suresh / Vectral Systems (Portland to Denver, In Progress), Joaquin Reyes / GreenStack Energy (Phoenix to Seattle, In Progress), Diego Castaneda / PivotPoint Labs (Brooklyn to Austin, In Progress), and Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics (Columbus to Seattle, In Progress).
- For each active move, use airtable_get_record (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", record_id: <id>) to retrieve the full Special Requirements field. This reveals the following specialty/hazmat items: (1) Keiko Tanaka has an Epilog Fusion Pro 48 industrial laser cutter (400 lbs, ~$35K value) containing a Class 3B CO2 laser tube (75W) requiring DOT 49 CFR Part 173 hazmat placarding, with an $800 hazmat handling surcharge and $450 interim storage fee. (2) Joaquin Reyes has a 900-lb rooftop solar panel testing rig (8ft x 4ft x 3ft) with sensitive calibration instruments requiring padded custom crating, with a critical scheduling conflict: GreenStack Seattle HQ building shutdown April 14-18 means NO building access, NO freight elevator, NO receiving dock. (3) Diego Castaneda has a Tormach PCNC 440 CNC mill (600 lbs, insured at $42,000) requiring vibration-dampened custom crating, climate-controlled transport (60-80F), and anti-static packaging. (4) Tyler Okonkwo has a carbon-frame road bike ($3,500) requiring professional bike boxing and a 65-inch TV requiring standard TV boxing. (5) Priya Suresh is relocating with an emotional support German Shepherd (Atlas, 85 lbs), requiring pet-friendly housing with a fenced yard or dog park where the landlord specifically approves 85+ lb dogs.
- Search emails using search_emails (query: "Keiko Tanaka" or "Mosaic" or "laser" or "hazmat") to find Chloe's email to Emeka about ops concerns, where she assigns vendor/coordinator allocations: Keiko Tanaka to Swift Relocations with Javier Morales coordinating. Also find Emeka's detailed account update email to David Chen listing Keiko's move details: $2,770 deal ($1,520 standard + $800 hazmat + $450 storage), 400-lb Epilog laser cutter with Class 3B hazmat placarding, 8-day lease gap (April 15-23), climate-controlled interim storage at Swift Seattle, and $2,450 in pending surcharges across Mosaic moves awaiting Tamara Byrd's written approval.
- Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages (query: "Keiko" or "Mosaic" or "laser" or "hazmat" or "DOT") to find Chloe's hazmat training implementation post in #operations explaining the new DOT documentation requirements: (a) shipping papers, (b) signed hazmat placarding certificate, (c) carrier PHMSA registration, (d) emergency response documentation. This post explicitly references the Keiko Tanaka incident as a case study for Thursday's training, noting that a missing placarding certificate for the Class 3B laser cutter shipment nearly resulted in a $50K+ federal fine. Also find Chloe's training invite follow-up tagging all coordinators including Javier.
- Search CRM deals using crm_search_deals (dealname: "Mosaic Tanaka" or similar) to find deal_mosaic_tanaka_apr2026 showing $2,770 total with surcharges pending client approval from Tamara Byrd, vendor Swift Relocations, pickup April 15-17, final delivery April 23+.
- Search Airtable using airtable_get_record (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", record_id: "recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle") to retrieve full details noting: Swift crew MUST sign laser equipment liability waiver acknowledging laser classification before pickup (Mosaic Equipment Handling Guidelines, page 7). Seattle delivery address is TBD (lease expected signed by April 10).
- Search emails using search_emails (query: "Joaquin" or "GreenStack" or "rig" or "solar") to find Chloe's email to Emeka about GreenStack relocations and vendor concerns, where she flags: Heartland Movers may not have equipment rated for the 900-lb rig (their Phoenix fleet typically handles up to 500-600 lbs and anything beyond that requires a flatbed with a liftgate). She suggests checking if Swift can handle this leg as well, since Swift's Seattle team is familiar with the GreenStack HQ building from the Venkatesh move. She also notes that putting both GreenStack relocations on Swift would place heavy load on one vendor in a tight timeframe.
- Use airtable_get_record (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", record_id: "recJoaquinReyesPhoenixSeattle") to identify the critical scheduling conflict: GreenStack Seattle office building shutdown April 14-18 for electrical grid upgrades. No building access, no freight elevator, no receiving dock during this window. Electrical contractor has exclusive site control per insurance requirements, absolutely no exceptions. Shutdown begins Monday April 14 at 6:00 AM. Access resumes Saturday April 19 at 8:00 AM, freight elevator available by 10:00 AM. The rig delivery MUST complete by EOD Sunday April 13 OR be scheduled for Saturday April 19 at 10:00 AM or later. Additionally, all invoices must be submitted by April 25 per GreenStack fiscal year-end.
- Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages (query: "Joaquin" or "GreenStack" or "rig" or "900") to find any updates from Chloe about vendor assignment and capacity checks.
- Search emails using search_emails (query: "Diego" or "PivotPoint" or "CNC" or "Castaneda") to find Catalina Dubois's internal update email to David Chen about PivotPoint Labs dual relocations, where she describes Diego's CNC mill as "the first piece of precision industrial equipment we've dealt with" and notes Javier is overseeing due to his expertise in heavy equipment documentation. Also find the CRM deal (deal_pivot_april_2026_dual) with $10,650 total for the dual relocation. Diego's specialty surcharges total ~$3,110: Custom Crating $1,200 + Climate Freight $850 + 16-day Interim Storage $640 + Cargo Insurance Rider $420. Diego's total portion including base cost is $6,630 (out of $10,650; the remaining $4,020 covers Nadia Okonjo's base + $500 walkup surcharge). Combined surcharges across both moves: $3,610.
- Use airtable_get_record (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", record_id: "recDiegoCastanedaBrooklynAustin") to retrieve full details noting: the 16-day storage gap (Brooklyn lease ends April 15, Austin apartment available May 1), the pre-move crating inspection request (Diego wants to be present, ideally April 10-11), and the requirement that storage must have loading dock or ground-floor access for the CNC mill (cannot go up ramps or staircases). Cargo insurance must cover minimum $50K per shipment.
- Look up CRM engagements for PivotPoint: first use crm_search_companies (name: "PivotPoint") to get company_id "company_pivot", then use crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_pivot"]) to find the engagement note (engagement_pivot_april_2026_dual_reloc) with Catalina's full account update including financials and the invoice INV-2026-0315 dated April 9 per client FY-end request.
- Search emails using search_emails (query: "Tyler Okonkwo" or "Canopy" or "Denver storage") to find Chloe's unread briefing email to Javier (email_chloe_javier_briefing_001) assigning Tyler's move and listing the primary action item: source a Denver-area short-term storage facility for ~450 cu ft (~2 weeks, April 14 to May 1), get a quote, and send it to Chloe and Catalina to finalize the invoice surcharge. Also find Catalina's comprehensive relocation plan email to Patricia Langford at Canopy Health with the $600 temporary storage surcharge (pending approval) and the full invoice breakdown for INV-2026-0401 ($9,210 total, $2,650 in surcharges pending).
- Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages (query: "Tyler" or "Okonkwo" or "Canopy" or "storage" or "April 12") to find Chloe's coordinator assignment post in #operations noting: Tyler Okonkwo assigned to Javier Morales, move dates April 12-14 (shifted from April 11-13 to resolve coordinator overlap), ~2-week temp storage gap in Denver (lease starts May 1), storage quote pending from Javier. Also find Chloe's Airtable update post listing the Denver temp storage quote as an outstanding blocker (needed by April 5).
- Search CRM deals using crm_search_deals (dealname: "Canopy Okonkwo" or similar) to find deal_canopy_okonkwo_apr2026 showing $2,160 deal amount with $600 temporary storage surcharge pending Patricia Langford's approval, and the date shift note confirming April 12-14 was accepted.
- Look up CRM engagements for Canopy: use crm_search_companies (name: "Canopy") to get company_id "company_canopy", then use crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_canopy"]) to find the surcharge approval flag engagement (eng_canopy_surcharge_approval_flag_001) showing that $600 (Tyler's storage) is one of three surcharges totaling $2,650 blocking vendor commitments, with a deadline of April 7 for approval.
- Use airtable_get_record (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", record_id: "recPriyaSureshPortlandDenver") to retrieve Priya Suresh's full record noting: relocating with emotional support German Shepherd named Atlas (85 lbs, ESA documentation on file), must source pet-friendly housing with fenced yard or nearby dog park where landlord specifically approves 85+ lb dogs. Preferred neighborhoods: RiNo (first choice) or Highlands. Household includes motorized standing desk (Uplift V2, ~80 lbs) and dual 27-inch monitor setup. Account Manager: Mina Hashimoto.
- Search emails using search_emails (query: "Priya Suresh" or "ESA" or "German Shepherd") for any housing search updates or vendor coordination related to this move.
- Search Airtable to find TWO records for Danielle Osei: one (recReloc00000004) showing status "Completed" with move dates April 26-27 and special requirements noting a 15-lb dog requiring pet-friendly housing, and another (rec_reloc_osei) showing status "Cancelled" with the note: "Client pulled relocation request due to budget reallocation. Cancellation email sent to Heartland (Jake Loomis) on 2/13. No acknowledgment received from vendor".
- Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues (query: "Danielle Osei" or "Osei") to find three active tickets: apartment search (issue_vectral_osei_apt, #310, state In Progress), flight booking (issue_vectral_osei_flight, #311, state Todo with note about pet-in-cabin rules), and moving coordination (issue_vectral_osei_movers, #312, state Todo). These tickets are still open despite the Cancelled Airtable record.
- Search emails using search_emails (query: "Danielle Osei" or "Osei") to find Javier's sent apartment search email to Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest requesting 3 pet-friendly options in Washington Park, Cherry Creek, or Highlands ($2,000-$2,600/month, 2BR).
- Search contacts using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Swift" or "Greg Pallone") to find Greg Pallone at Swift Relocations (greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com), the vendor handling Keiko Tanaka's hazmat move, Kevin Tran's completed move, and potentially Joaquin Reyes's rig if Heartland cannot handle the weight.
- Search contacts using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Heartland" or "Lisa Kwan") to find Lisa Kwan at Heartland Movers (lisa.kwan@heartlandmovers.com), the primary vendor contact in the contacts database. Note: Jake Loomis is also referenced in emails and Slack as a Heartland contact (cancellation emails for Marchetti/Osei, cold-chain capacity calls), but he does not appear in the Contacts service.
- Search contacts using contacts_search_contacts (query: "UrbanNest" or "Carmen Reyes") to find Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest Solutions (carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com), the apartment sourcing partner used for Denver relocations.
- Search contacts using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Atlas Corporate" or "Michael Stern") to find Michael Stern at Atlas Corporate Travel (michael.stern@atlascorporatetravel.com), the flight booking vendor.
- Use airtable_get_record for each remaining active move (Rachel Dominguez recReloc00000000029, Sasha Greenwald recReloc00000000034, Jordan Whitfield recReloc00000000035) and optionally linear_list_issues to confirm these moves do not involve DOT-regulated materials or specialty equipment: Rachel Dominguez / Canopy Health (LA to Portland, standard 1BR with $2,000 equipment stipend), Sasha Greenwald / PivotPoint Labs (NY to Nashville, standard 1BR/studio with $1,900 stipend), and Jordan Whitfield / BrightLoop Analytics (Minneapolis to Denver, standard 1BR with $1,800 stipend).
- Search emails using search_emails (query: "hazmat training" or "DOT" or "mandatory training" or "April 24") to find Chloe's training invite email listing: date Thursday April 24 at 2:00-2:30 PM Pacific on Zoom, required attendees including Javier Morales, agenda covering DOT regulatory overview, new documentation checklist walkthrough, Keiko Tanaka case study, and Marcus Thorne covering financial exposure (up to $59,017 per violation per day under 49 CFR 107.329, $50K contingent liability booked). Pre-work required: review all in-progress relocations for DOT-regulated materials and send findings to Chloe before Thursday.
- Search Slack messages to find Chloe's detailed hazmat implementation plan post in #operations outlining three initiatives: (1) DOT Hazmat Documentation Checklist Template with four categories (shipping papers, hazmat placarding certificate, carrier PHMSA registration, emergency response documentation), (2) Airtable Verification Gate adding a mandatory "DOT Docs Complete" checkbox to tblRelocations01 that must be checked before any hazmat relocation can be marked Completed, and (3) the mandatory training session on April 24.
- Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria filtering by client names: "Mosaic", "GreenStack", "PivotPoint", "Canopy" or similar) to find line-item surcharge breakdowns. Key invoices: INV-2026-0411 for Mosaic ($9,010, includes $800 hazmat handling + $450 interim storage for Keiko Tanaka), INV-2026-0418 for GreenStack ($9,440, includes $1,800 hazmat transport surcharge for Mei-Lin Zhao + $950 overweight equipment surcharge for Joaquin Reyes + $150 interim hazmat storage), INV-2026-0315 for PivotPoint ($10,650, includes $1,200 custom crating + $850 climate freight + $640 storage + $420 cargo insurance for Diego Castaneda), INV-2026-0401 for Canopy ($9,210, includes $600 temporary storage surcharge for Tyler Okonkwo pending approval).
- Create one Linear issue using linear_create_issue (team: "Operations", title: "DOT/Compliance Gaps Summary — Javier Morales Active Moves" or similar, assignee: Javier Morales, priority: urgent) summarizing all open DOT and compliance gaps across Javier's moves to bring to Thursday's hazmat training. The issue should cover: Keiko Tanaka's Class 3B laser cutter requiring hazmat placarding per DOT 49 CFR Part 173 with missing/pending placarding certificate and four DOT documentation items per Chloe's new checklist; Joaquin Reyes's 900-lb rig with vendor capacity concern (Heartland may not handle weight) and building shutdown conflict (April 14-18); Diego Castaneda's CNC mill requiring compliance-level attention for crating and transport; and any other gaps identified during the audit. The prompt specifies "create one Linear issue" — a single summary issue, not individual issues per move.

Also find Chloe's Airtable update post listing the Denver temp storage quote as an outstanding blocker (needed by April 5).
- Search CRM deals using crm_search_deals (dealname: "Canopy Okonkwo" or similar) to find deal_canopy_okonkwo_apr2026 showing $2,160 deal amount with $600 temporary storage surcharge pending Patricia Langford's approval, and the date shift note confirming April 12-14 was accepted.
- Look up CRM engagements for Canopy: use crm_search_companies (name: "Canopy") to get company_id "company_canopy", then use crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_canopy"]) to find the surcharge approval flag engagement (eng_canopy_surcharge_approval_flag_001) showing that $600 (Tyler's storage) is one of three surcharges totaling $2,650 blocking vendor commitments, with a deadline of April 7 for approval.
- Use airtable_get_record (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", record_id: "recPriyaSureshPortlandDenver") to retrieve Priya Suresh's full record noting: relocating with emotional support German Shepherd named Atlas (85 lbs, ESA documentation on file), must source pet-friendly housing with fenced yard or nearby dog park where landlord specifically approves 85+ lb dogs. Preferred neighborhoods: RiNo (first choice) or Highlands. Household includes motorized standing desk (Uplift V2, ~80 lbs) and dual 27-inch monitor setup. Account Manager: Mina Hashimoto.

Cancellation email sent to Heartland (Jake Loomis) on 2/13. No acknowledgment received from vendor".

These tickets are still open despite the Cancelled Airtable record.

Note: Jake Loomis is also referenced in emails and Slack as a Heartland contact (cancellation emails for Marchetti/Osei, cold-chain capacity calls), but he does not appear in the Contacts service.
- Search contacts using contacts_search_contacts (query: "UrbanNest" or "Carmen Reyes") to find Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest Solutions (carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com), the apartment sourcing partner used for Denver relocations.

- Search emails using search_emails (query: "hazmat training" or "DOT" or "mandatory training" or "April 24") to find Chloe's training invite email listing: date Thursday April 24 at 2:00-2:30 PM Pacific on Zoom, required attendees including Javier Morales, agenda covering DOT regulatory overview, new documentation checklist walkthrough, Keiko Tanaka case study, and Marcus Thorne covering financial exposure (up to $59,017 per violation per day under 49 CFR 107.329, $50K contingent liability booked). Pre-work required: review all in-progress relocations for DOT-regulated materials and send findings to Chloe before Thursday.
- Search Slack messages to find Chloe's detailed hazmat implementation plan post in #operations outlining three initiatives: (1) DOT Hazmat Documentation Checklist Template with four categories (shipping papers, hazmat placarding certificate, carrier PHMSA registration, emergency response documentation), (2) Airtable Verification Gate adding a mandatory "DOT Docs Complete" checkbox to tblRelocations01 that must be checked before any hazmat relocation can be marked Completed, and (3) the mandatory training session on April 24.
- Search QuickBooks invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria filtering by client names: "Mosaic", "GreenStack", "PivotPoint", "Canopy" or similar) to find line-item surcharge breakdowns. Key invoices: INV-2026-0411 for Mosaic ($9,010, includes $800 hazmat handling + $450 interim storage for Keiko Tanaka), INV-2026-0418 for GreenStack ($9,440, includes $1,800 hazmat transport surcharge for Mei-Lin Zhao + $950 overweight equipment surcharge for Joaquin Reyes + $150 interim hazmat storage), INV-2026-0315 for PivotPoint ($10,650, includes $1,200 custom crating + $850 climate freight + $640 storage + $420 cargo insurance for Diego Castaneda), INV-2026-0401 for Canopy ($9,210, includes $600 temporary storage surcharge for Tyler Okonkwo pending approval).
- Create one Linear issue using linear_create_issue (team: "Operations", title: "DOT/Compliance Gaps Summary — Javier Morales Active Moves" or similar, assignee: Javier Morales, priority: urgent) summarizing all open DOT and compliance gaps across Javier's moves to bring to Thursday's hazmat training. The issue should cover: Keiko Tanaka's Class 3B laser cutter requiring hazmat placarding per DOT 49 CFR Part 173 with missing/pending placarding certificate and four DOT documentation items per Chloe's new checklist; Joaquin Reyes's 900-lb rig with vendor capacity concern (Heartland may not handle weight) and building shutdown conflict (April 14-18); Diego Castaneda's CNC mill requiring compliance-level attention for crating and transport; and any other gaps identified during the audit. The prompt specifies "create one Linear issue" — a single summary issue, not individual issues per move.
